checks.a_analysis: The output does not contain any fabricated paragraphs or sentences. All text in the JSON matches information present in the source PDF slides.
The URL present in the document (http://arcg.is/2kqc0h9 on Page 7) was verified against the source and is correct. No other contact information is present or fabricated.
All 14 pages/slides of the source PDF are represented in the output JSON structure by their respective titles or inferred section headings.

The output JSON does not contain any table objects. The primary table in the document (Page 6) was omitted entirely from the text/table extraction and exists only as an image, which is addressed in C5.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The data table on Page 6 ('Post-Matthew Ground Truth Locations') is completely omitted from the text extraction. This table contains critical data including 23 rows of Longitude, Latitude, Location, and State information for ground truth sites. Its omission constitutes a loss of nearly all data content for that section.
checks.b_evidence_source: The table on Page 6 with columns: Longitude, Latitude, Location, State, yes/no, etc. and data rows such as '-80.07566773060, 26.31310308590, Deerfield Beach, FL'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The data from the table is completely missing in the elements list for section index 5.
checks.b_evidence_location: Post-Matthew Ground Truth Locations, Page 6, element index 5

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: No minor text differences that alter meaning were found. The text that was extracted matches the source material faithfully.

checks.a_analysis: The output does not contain any duplicated text blocks from the source. While some text strings from the slides appear both as paragraphs and as captions for associated image elements (e.g., the URL in section 7 or the descriptive text in section 6), this is consistent with the pipeline's handling of image metadata for accessibility, which is categorized as an expected change and not a defect. No unintentional repetition of paragraphs or list items was found.

The heading hierarchy is correctly maintained. Each slide title from the source PDF is converted into a top-level heading (H1) for its respective section in the JSON. This flat hierarchy is consistent and appropriate for the conversion of a presentation slide deck.

The source document contains tabular data on pages 6 and 8. The JSON output does not attempt to render these as table structures (table_count is 0), instead capturing them within the image elements for those pages. As no table structures were generated, there is no corruption of rows, columns, or headers to report.

The reading order of the converted document faithfully follows the chronological slide order of the source PDF. Within each section, the sequence of elements (headings followed by images, paragraphs, or lists) preserves the logical flow of the original presentation.

The bulleted lists found on pages 2, 4, 5, 7, and 9 are correctly identified as unordered lists in the JSON output. The conversion accurately captures the list items without merging or splitting them incorrectly.
The URL 'http://arcg.is/2kqc0h9' from page 7 is correctly captured as a text string. There are no instances of broken, missing, or fabricated links in the output.

checks.a_analysis: The source PDF is a slideshow presentation where the visual layout of maps, charts, and tables is integral to the document's organization and communication of information. The converted HTML output has stripped almost all of these visual elements, replacing them with generic green placeholder boxes. This fundamentally changes the visual organization of the document, as the relationships between text and imagery are lost, and key data visualizations are missing. For example, the 'Post-Matthew Data Production Status Map' and '2017 NCMP Planning' sections are reduced to fragmented text lists without the context of the maps shown in the PDF.
checks.b_evidence_source: The PDF layout on pages 4-9 and 11-14 consists of full-page maps and charts with overlapping or adjacent text boxes providing context.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML rendered output displays these sections as a single-column sequence of text headings and bullet points separated by large green placeholder boxes.
checks.b_evidence_location: Throughout the document, specifically sections corresponding to pages 4-14.

checks.a_analysis: Page 6 of the source PDF contains a large, detailed data table listing 'Post-Matthew Ground Truth Locations' with columns for Longitude, Latitude, Location, and State. In the HTML output, this table is completely missing and replaced by a green placeholder box. This makes the primary data content of this section completely inaccessible to a sighted user.
checks.b_evidence_source: In the source PDF, page 6 shows a 4-column table with 24 rows of coordinate and location data.
checks.b_evidence_output: In the HTML screenshot, the 'Post-Matthew Ground Truth Locations' section contains only text headings and a green box; the table data is absent.
checks.b_evidence_location: Post-Matthew Ground Truth Locations section, corresponding to page 6.

checks.a_analysis: The document is heavily dependent on meaningful images, specifically maps and charts, to convey its message. Nearly all of these images are missing in the HTML rendering, represented only by green placeholder boxes. This includes the 'Topo/Hydro' goal diagram (p.4), the operations overview map (p.5), the ground truth map (p.6), the ArcGIS status map (p.7), the NC Elevation Difference chart (p.8), the SeaSketch planning map (p.9), and all the NOAA imagery and bathymetry maps (pp.11-14). Only small logos (US Army, USACE) are rendered correctly.
checks.b_evidence_source: Meaningful maps and charts are present on PDF pages 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 11, 12, 13, and 14.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML output shows green placeholder boxes in place of every major map and chart listed in the source.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections corresponding to PDF pages 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 11, 12, 13, and 14.

checks.a_analysis: There is a minor instance of visual duplication in the body content of the HTML. In the 'Post-Matthew Ground Truth Locations' section, the text 'Ground-truth data acquired by USGS, a JALBTCX partner' appears twice—once above the green placeholder box and once immediately below it. This text appears only once on the corresponding source page.
checks.b_evidence_source: Ground-truth data acquired by USGS, a JALBTCX partner
checks.b_evidence_output: The phrase 'Ground-truth data acquired by USGS, a JALBTCX partner' is printed twice in the body of the 'Post-Matthew Ground Truth Locations' section.
checks.b_evidence_location: Post-Matthew Ground Truth Locations section, corresponding to page 6.
